claim: The /team task assign <id> <agent> slash command does not check isTaskBlocked() before assigning a task. The BUG-1 fix added blocked checks to claimTask() and the task_assign tool action, but this third assignment path was missed. A blocked task can be assigned and started via the slash command.
evidence: leader-task-commands.ts:168-176 — updateTask sets owner and status=pending without any isTaskBlocked check. Compare with leader-teams-tool.ts task_assign action which now has the BUG-1 fix.
runtimeTrigger: Leader runs /team task assign 3 agent1 where task #3 is blocked by unfinished task #2. The task is assigned and the worker receives a task_assignment message, starting work on a blocked task.

claim: The interactive panel's assignTask callback does not check isTaskBlocked() before assigning a task via the UI. Same class as BUG-1/BUG-7 — a third unguarded assignment path. A blocked task can be reassigned through the panel.
evidence: leader.ts openInteractiveWidget assignTask callback — calls updateTask to set owner without any blocked check. Compare with leader-teams-tool.ts task_assign which now checks isTaskBlocked.
runtimeTrigger: Leader opens the panel (/tw), selects a blocked task, and reassigns it to a worker via the panel UI. The task is assigned despite having unfinished dependencies.

claim: claimNextAvailableTask checks isTaskBlocked before calling claimTask, but claimTask now also checks isTaskBlocked by default (BUG-1 fix). This causes a redundant double-check of blocked status on every claim attempt, adding unnecessary filesystem reads. claimNextAvailableTask should pass checkBlocked: false to skip the redundant inner check.
evidence: task-store.ts claimNextAvailableTask: calls isTaskBlocked at line ~780, then calls claimTask without checkBlocked: false, which triggers a second isTaskBlocked check inside claimTask.
runtimeTrigger: Every auto-claim cycle reads dependency tasks twice — once in claimNextAvailableTask and once inside claimTask. With many tasks and dependencies, this doubles filesystem I/O on the hot path.
